title: Macroglena idonea
taxonomy: Macroglena idonea is a species of liverwort, which is a type of non-vascular land plant. Liverworts are classified under the division Marchantiophyta. They are further divided into several classes, including the class Jungermanniopsida, to which Macroglena idonea belongs. The class Jungermanniopsida is characterized by the presence of perianths and the lack of a rhizome. Macroglena idonea is a member of the family Calypogeiaceae and the genus Macroglena.

Its extracts have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. In some parts of the world, it is used as a treatment for inflammation and wound healing. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential side effects. Its medicinal uses are still being studied, and it is not widely recognized as a treatment in conventional medicine.
